  • Method Details

    • decompressFile

      public static File decompressFile(File compressedFile) throws IOException, CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      Tries to decompress a file into a newly created temporary directory, trying gzip or zip regardless of the filename or its suffix.
      Returns:
      folder containing all decompressed files
      Throws:
      IOException
      CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType

    • decompressFile

      public static List<File> decompressFile(File directory, File compressedFile) throws IOException, CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      Defaults keeping subDirectories to false.
      Throws:
      IOException
      CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      See Also:
      • decompressFile(File, File, String, boolean)

    • decompressFile

      public static List<File> decompressFile(File directory, File compressedFile, String unzippedFileName) throws IOException, CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      Defaults keeping subDirectories to false, but provides unzipped file name.
      Throws:
      IOException
      CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      See Also:
      • decompressFile(File, File, String, boolean)

    • decompressFile

      public static List<File> decompressFile(File directory, File compressedFile, boolean keepSubdirectories) throws IOException, CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      Defaults keeping subDirectories to false and unzipped file name to null.
      Throws:
      IOException
      CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      See Also:
      • decompressFile(File, File, String, boolean)

    • decompressFile

      public static List<File> decompressFile(File directory, File compressedFile, String unzippedFileName, boolean keepSubdirectories) throws IOException, CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType
      Tries to decompress a file using TAR+gzip, gzip, TAR or Zip regardless of the filename or its suffix.
      Parameters:
      directory - directory where archive's contents will be decompressed to
      compressedFile - compressed file
      unzippedFileName - unzipped file name (in case of one file)
      Returns:
      list of files that have been extracted or null an empty list if archive couldn't be decompressed
      Throws:
      IOException - if problem occurred reading compressed file, or directory couldn't be written to
      CompressionUtil.UnsupportedCompressionType - if the compression type wasn't recognized

    • isGzipFormat

      public static boolean isGzipFormat(File compressedFile) throws IOException
      Check the file's first two bytes, to see if they are the gzip magic number.
      Parameters:
      compressedFile - compressed file
      Returns:
      true if the file is in gzip format
      Throws:
      IOException - if a problem occurred reading compressed file

    • isTarFormat

      public static boolean isTarFormat(File compressedFile) throws IOException
      Check the file is a Tape ARchive (TAR).
      Parameters:
      compressedFile - compressed file
      Returns:
      true if the file is a TAR
      Throws:
      IOException - if a problem occurred reading compressed file

    • untgzFile

      public static List<File> untgzFile(File directory, File tgzFile) throws IOException
      Extracts a gzipped TAR file. Directory structure and hidden files (i.e. files starting with a dot) are ignored.
      Parameters:
      directory - where the file should be extracted to
      tgzFile - to extract
      Returns:
      a list of all created files
      Throws:
      IOException

    • untarFile

      public static List<File> untarFile(File directory, File tarFile) throws IOException
      Extracts a plain TAR file. Directory structure and hidden files (i.e. files starting with a dot) are ignored.
      Parameters:
      directory - where the file should be extracted to
      tarFile - to extract
      Returns:
      a list of all created files
      Throws:
      IOException

    • ungzipFile

      public static List<File> ungzipFile(File directory, File gzipFile, boolean isTarred) throws IOException
      Gunzip a file. Use this method with isTarred false if the gzip contains a single file. If it's a gzip of a TAR pass true to isTarred (or call untgzFile(File, File) which is what this method just redirects to for isTarred).
      Parameters:
      directory - the output directory for the uncompressed file(s)
      gzipFile - the gzip file
      isTarred - true if the gzip contains a TAR
      Returns:
      a List of the uncompressed file name(s)
      Throws:
      IOException - if reading or writing fails

    • ungzipFile

      public static List<File> ungzipFile(File directory, File gzipFile, String unzippedName, boolean isTarred) throws IOException
      Gunzip a file. Similar to ungzipFile(File, File, boolean), but with ability to control unzipped file name.
      Parameters:
      directory - the output directory for the uncompressed file(s)
      gzipFile - the gzip file
      unzippedName - the unzipped file
      isTarred - true if the gzip contains a TAR
      Returns:
      a List of the uncompressed file name(s)
      Throws:
      IOException - if reading or writing fails

    • unzipFile

      public static List<File> unzipFile(File directory, File zipFile) throws IOException
      Defaults keepSubdirectories to false.
      Throws:
      IOException
      See Also:
      • unzipFile(java.io.File, java.io.File, boolean)

    • zipDir

      public static void zipDir(File dir, File zipFile) throws IOException
      Zip a directory with all files but skipping included subdirectories. Only files directly within the directory are added to the archive.
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to zip
      zipFile - the zipped file
      Throws:
      IOException

    • zipDir

      public static void zipDir(File dir, File zipFile, boolean inclSubdirs) throws IOException
      Zip a directory with all files. Files in Subdirectories will be included if the inclSubdirs is true.
      Parameters:
      dir - the directory to zip
      zipFile - the zipped file
      inclSubdirs - if true includes all subdirectories recursively
      Throws:
      IOException

    • zipFile

      public static void zipFile(File file, File zipFile) throws IOException
      Throws:
      IOException

    • zipFiles

      public static void zipFiles(Collection<File> files, File rootContext, File zipFile) throws IOException
      Creates a zip archive from a given collection of files. In order to preserve paths in the archive a rootContext can be specified which will be removed from the individual zip entries. For example a rootContext of /home/freak with a file /home/freak/photo/birthday.jpg to be zipped will result in a zip entry with a path photo/birthday.jpg.
      Parameters:
      files - to be included in the zip archive
      rootContext - optional path to be removed from each file
      zipFile - the zip file to be created
      Throws:
      IOException

    • unzipFile

      public static List<File> unzipFile(File directory, File zipFile, boolean keepSubdirectories) throws IOException
      Extracts a zipped file into a target directory. If the file is wrapped in a root directory, this is removed by default. Other subdirectories are ignored according to the parameter keepSubdirectories.
      The following types of files are also ignored by default: i) hidden files (i.e. files starting with a dot) ii) Apple resource fork (__MACOSX), including its subdirectories and subfiles
      Parameters:
      directory - where the zipped file and its subdirectories should be extracted to
      zipFile - to extract
      keepSubdirectories - whether to preserve subdirectories or not
      Returns:
      a list of all created files and directories extracted to target directory
      Throws:
      IOException
